perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video starts with a man sitting in front of a white table, wearing a white T-shirt and a gray hat, talking to the camera. (2) He introduces and displays four different brands of screen protectors: his own brand (Diamond Shield), MAGEASY, and imos. (3) He proceeds to demonstrate the screen protectors by peeling off an existing one from a smartphone and then rapidly applying and removing protectors from the showcased brands to show they leave no residue or damage. (4) The video concludes with the man showing the clean phone screen and then a full-screen text graphic appearing with a summary message.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) A male voice begins speaking in Traditional Chinese with an energetic and authoritative tone. (2) Low-volume, upbeat electronic background music starts and continues throughout the video. (3) During the demonstration, distinct sound effects (SFX) of plastic peeling and the phone being tapped against the table are heard. (4) The music remains consistent until the end of the video, ending with the speaker's final remarks.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: Traditional Chinese subtitles appear at the bottom, translating the speaker's words. (2) Hook Text: Top-left corner displays social media handles '3c有意思tim哥' and '3ctim'. (3) Decorative/Effect Text: A comment box from a user is shown briefly during the intro. (4) Functional Text: A large block of text appears at the end summarizing that Apple has not officially forbidden screen protectors and reputable brands are safe to use.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Hook -> Product Showcase -> Practical Demonstration -> Summary and Conclusion

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 male main creator present throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Adult male wearing a gray flat cap, glasses, a white t-shirt with a cartoon graphic, a yellow watch, and several beaded bracelets.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A simple indoor setting with a plain white wall and a white desk covered with a patterned table runner.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to debunk a rumor about iPhone 17 (likely a future model reference) screen protectors causing damage. The creator uses a rapid-fire demonstration with several high-quality brands to prove that reputable protectors do not leave residue or harm the display, thereby reassuring consumers.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The speaker explicitly presents several brands and tests them, including his own brand, while giving positive feedback on their quality.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Signature flat cap, opening greeting '大家好 Tim哥'.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video targets tech-savvy consumers in the Chinese-speaking market, specifically focusing on early-adopter concerns regarding new smartphone hardware.
